battle-megiddo-1457bc.war: Thutmose III's first campaign in the Levant
battle-megiddo-1457bc.gloss: Thutmose III defeated a Canaanite coalition and secured Egyptian dominance in the Levant. The campaign is one of the earliest battles described in substantial detail.

battle-kadesh-1274bc.outcome: Indecisive; Egyptian tactical survival and Hittite strategic check
battle-kadesh-1274bc.gloss: Ramesses II and Muwatalli II fought one of the largest chariot battles of the Bronze Age. Its propaganda, diplomacy, and later treaty made it as famous as its battlefield result.

battle-marathon-490bc.gloss: Athenian and Plataean hoplites defeated a Persian expeditionary force. The victory strengthened Athenian confidence and became a lasting symbol of Greek resistance.

battle-thermopylae-480bc.gloss: A small Greek rearguard delayed Xerxes' army at a narrow pass. The defeat became famous for disciplined resistance and bought time for wider Greek defense.

battle-salamis-480bc.gloss: The Greek fleet lured the larger Persian navy into confined waters and broke its offensive power. Salamis shifted the invasion toward Persian retreat.

battle-gaugamela-331bc.gloss: Alexander defeated Darius III in the decisive battle of the Persian campaign. The victory opened the imperial heartland to Macedonian control.

siege-tyre-332bc.gloss: Alexander used siege works and naval pressure to take the island city of Tyre. The operation displayed his willingness to solve strategic problems with engineering.

battle-cannae-216bc.gloss: Hannibal enveloped a much larger Roman army and destroyed it in a classic double envelopment. Cannae became a benchmark for tactical annihilation.

battle-zama-202bc.gloss: Scipio Africanus defeated Hannibal and ended Carthage's chance to dominate the western Mediterranean. The result forced a harsh peace on Carthage.
battle-zama-202bc.casualties: About 1,500 Roman dead; about 20,000 Carthaginian dead and 20,000 captured

siege-alesia-52bc.gloss: Caesar trapped Vercingetorix with siege lines while holding off a relief army. Alesia effectively ended organized Gallic resistance to Roman conquest.

battle-carrhae-53bc.gloss: Parthian horse archers and cataphracts destroyed Crassus' Roman army. The defeat exposed Roman vulnerability beyond the Euphrates and shook Roman politics.

combatants: Forces of Mark Antony and Cleopatra
battle-actium-31bc.war: Final War of the Roman Republic
battle-actium-31bc.gloss: Octavian's fleet defeated Antony and Cleopatra near western Greece. The victory cleared the way for Octavian to become Augustus and found the Roman Empire.

series.label: Final War of the Roman Republic

battle-teutoburg-forest-ad9.gloss: Arminius ambushed and destroyed three Roman legions in forested terrain. The disaster helped halt Rome's permanent expansion east of the Rhine.

combatants: Sun Quan and Liu Bei coalition
battle-red-cliffs-ad208.war: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty
battle-red-cliffs-ad208.gloss: A southern coalition stopped Cao Cao's northern army in a river battle remembered for fire ships and strategy. The result helped shape the Three Kingdoms balance.

series.label: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty

battle-adrianople-378.gloss: Gothic forces defeated and killed Emperor Valens. Adrianople exposed deep Roman military pressures and marked a major shock to imperial authority.

battle-yarmouk-636.gloss: The Rashidun army defeated a Byzantine field force and secured the conquest of Syria. Yarmouk transformed the political and religious map of the eastern Mediterranean.

battle-qadisiyyah-636.gloss: Arab Muslim forces defeated the Sasanian army in Mesopotamia. The battle opened the road toward Ctesiphon and accelerated the collapse of Sasanian power.

battle-tours-732.gloss: Charles Martel's Frankish army stopped an Umayyad raiding force in western Gaul. Later memory made Tours a defining episode in early medieval Europe.

battle-talas-751.gloss: Abbasid forces defeated a Tang army in Central Asia. The battle signaled the limits of Tang influence westward and became linked to wider cultural exchange.

battle-hastings-1066.gloss: William of Normandy defeated Harold II and ended Anglo-Saxon rule in England. The result reshaped English aristocracy, law, language, and landholding.

battle-manzikert-1071.gloss: Seljuk forces captured Emperor Romanos IV and weakened Byzantine control in Anatolia. The defeat helped open the region to Turkish settlement and power.

battle-hattin-1187.gloss: Saladin destroyed the main crusader field army near the Horns of Hattin. The victory led directly to the capture of Jerusalem and the Third Crusade.

battle-mohi-1241.gloss: Mongol commanders used mobility, feigned retreat, and coordinated attacks to break the Hungarian army. The battle devastated Hungary during the western Mongol campaign.

battle-ain-jalut-1260.gloss: The Mamluks defeated a Mongol force in Palestine and checked Mongol expansion into Egypt. Ain Jalut became a landmark in the defense of the Islamic Near East.

battle-yamen-1279.gloss: The Yuan defeated the last Southern Song fleet, ending the dynasty's resistance. The battle completed Mongol rule over China under Kublai Khan.

battle-bach-dang-1288.gloss: Tran Hung Dao's forces trapped a Yuan fleet among concealed stakes in the tidal river. The victory ended the third Mongol invasion of Dai Viet.

battle-grunwald-1410.gloss: A Polish-Lithuanian coalition defeated the Teutonic Order in one of medieval Europe's largest battles. The result weakened the order's military and political position.

battle-agincourt-1415.gloss: Henry V's army defeated French forces on muddy ground with disciplined archery and defensive positioning. Agincourt became central to English military memory.

siege-constantinople-1453.gloss: Mehmed II captured Constantinople after a major artillery-backed siege. The city's fall ended the Byzantine Empire and made Istanbul the Ottoman capital.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-towton-1461.gloss: Yorkist forces won a brutal battle that helped secure Edward IV's claim to the English throne. Towton is remembered as one of England's bloodiest medieval battles.

siege-tenochtitlan-1521.war: Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire
siege-tenochtitlan-1521.gloss: Spanish and Indigenous allied forces besieged and captured the Mexica capital. The fall of Tenochtitlan remade power in central Mexico and began New Spain.

series.label: Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire

battle-pavia-1525.gloss: Imperial forces defeated and captured Francis I of France. Pavia showed the power of firearms and infantry coordination in early modern European war.

battle-panipat-1526.gloss: Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi with field artillery and mobile tactics. The battle established Mughal power in northern India.

battle-mohacs-1526.gloss: Suleiman's army shattered the Hungarian royal host and King Louis II died in the retreat. The defeat opened Hungary to Ottoman and Habsburg rivalry.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-nagashino-1575.gloss: Oda and Tokugawa forces defeated Takeda cavalry near Nagashino Castle. The battle is famous for defensive works and the effective use of arquebusiers.

battle-lepanto-1571.gloss: A Holy League fleet defeated Ottoman naval forces in a vast galley battle. Lepanto checked Ottoman naval momentum in the central Mediterranean.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-alcacer-quibir-1578.gloss: Moroccan forces defeated Portugal's invasion and King Sebastian was killed. The disaster triggered a Portuguese succession crisis and altered Iberian politics.

battle-sekigahara-1600.gloss: Tokugawa Ieyasu defeated a rival coalition in a decisive struggle for national control. Sekigahara cleared the path to the Tokugawa shogunate.

siege-osaka-1614.gloss: Tokugawa forces destroyed the Toyotomi stronghold after winter and summer campaigns. Osaka ended the last major challenge to Tokugawa rule.

battle-breitenfeld-1631.gloss: Gustavus Adolphus defeated the Catholic League and transformed the war's momentum. Breitenfeld displayed flexible Swedish tactics and revived Protestant fortunes.

battle-rocroi-1643.gloss: French forces broke the Spanish tercios and announced France's rising military power. Rocroi became a symbol of the transition away from Spanish battlefield dominance.

battle-vienna-1683.gloss: A relief army led by John III Sobieski broke the Ottoman siege of Vienna. The victory marked a major turning point in Habsburg-Ottoman conflict.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-blenheim-1704.gloss: Marlborough and Eugene defeated Franco-Bavarian forces and saved Vienna from pressure. Blenheim ended the aura of French invincibility under Louis XIV.

combatants: Nawab of Bengal and French allies
battle-plassey-1757.gloss: Company forces under Robert Clive defeated the Nawab of Bengal after elite defections. Plassey opened the way for British political power in Bengal.
battle-plassey-1757.casualties: British: 22 killed and 50 wounded; Nawab's army: over 500 casualties

siege-yorktown-1781.gloss: Cornwallis surrendered after American and French forces trapped his army by land and sea. Yorktown ended major British land operations in North America.
siege-yorktown-1781.casualties: Allies: about 400 killed or wounded; British and German forces: about 600 killed or wounded and about 7,600 surrendered

battle-austerlitz-1805.location: Near Slavkov u Brna, Czech Republic
battle-austerlitz-1805.gloss: Napoleon defeated the Russian and Austrian emperors through deception and a central counterstroke. Austerlitz is often treated as his tactical masterpiece.

battle-trafalgar-1805.gloss: Nelson's fleet destroyed Franco-Spanish naval power while he was mortally wounded. Trafalgar secured British command of the seas during the Napoleonic Wars.

battle-borodino-1812.gloss: Napoleon won a costly battle on the road to Moscow, but failed to destroy the Russian army. Borodino showed the attritional danger of the Russian campaign.

battle-leipzig-1813.gloss: Coalition armies defeated Napoleon in the largest battle of the Napoleonic Wars. Leipzig forced the French retreat from Germany and weakened the empire decisively.

battle-waterloo-1815.gloss: Wellington and Blucher defeated Napoleon's final army. Waterloo ended the Hundred Days and closed the Napoleonic era.

battle-ayacucho-1824.gloss: Patriot forces defeated the main Spanish royalist army in Peru. Ayacucho effectively ended Spanish military power on the South American mainland.

combatants: France, Britain, Ottoman Empire, and Sardinia
siege-sevastopol-1854.gloss: Allied armies besieged Russia's Black Sea naval base for nearly a year. The campaign became a defining episode of the Crimean War and modern siege warfare.

battle-solferino-1859.gloss: A vast and chaotic battle helped push Austria from Lombardy. The suffering witnessed at Solferino inspired Henry Dunant and the Red Cross movement.

battle-gettysburg-1863.gloss: Union forces stopped Lee's invasion of Pennsylvania after three days of fighting. Gettysburg became the Civil War's symbolic turning point in the eastern theater.

siege-vicksburg-1863.gloss: Grant's campaign and siege forced the surrender of Vicksburg. The victory gave the Union control of the Mississippi River.

combatants: Austrian Empire and Kingdom of Saxony
battle-koniggratz-1866.gloss: Prussia defeated Austria in a decisive battle shaped by rail mobilization, staff planning, and modern rifles. The result shifted German leadership toward Prussia.

battle-isandlwana-1879.gloss: Zulu forces overwhelmed a British camp in one of Britain's worst colonial defeats. The battle showed the danger of underestimating local armies and terrain.

battle-adwa-1896.gloss: Ethiopia defeated an Italian invasion and preserved its independence during the Scramble for Africa. Adwa became a major symbol of anti-colonial resistance.

battle-omdurman-1898.gloss: Kitchener's army used rifles, artillery, and machine guns to defeat Mahdist forces. Omdurman illustrated the lethal imbalance of late imperial warfare.

siege-port-arthur-1904.gloss: Japanese forces captured Russia's fortified naval base after costly assaults and siege operations. Port Arthur signaled Japan's arrival as a major military power.

battle-mukden-1905.gloss: Japan defeated Russian armies in a huge land battle in Manchuria. Mukden helped force Russia toward peace and shocked observers of imperial power.

battle-tsushima-1905.gloss: Japan destroyed Russia's Baltic Fleet after its long voyage to East Asia. Tsushima was a decisive naval victory and a landmark in modern sea power.

battle-tannenberg-1914.gloss: German forces encircled and destroyed a Russian army in East Prussia. Tannenberg made Hindenburg and Ludendorff famous and stabilized Germany's eastern front.

battle-marne-1914.gloss: French and British forces stopped the German advance toward Paris. The battle ended hopes for a quick German victory and led toward trench warfare.

gallipoli-campaign-1915.gloss: Ottoman defenders defeated an Allied attempt to force the Dardanelles and open a route to Russia. Gallipoli shaped Turkish, Australian, and New Zealand memory.

battle-verdun-1916.gloss: Verdun became a grinding battle of endurance between France and Germany. Its scale, duration, and slogan of resistance made it a defining symbol of World War I.

battle-somme-1916.outcome: Allied strategic pressure with limited territorial gains
battle-somme-1916.gloss: The Somme combined mass infantry attacks, artillery, and early tank use in a costly campaign. It remains a central image of World War I attrition.

battle-passchendaele-1917.outcome: Allied tactical victory with severe losses
battle-passchendaele-1917.gloss: Fighting around Ypres sank into mud, artillery, and attrition. Passchendaele became shorthand for the suffering and disputed gains of trench warfare.
battle-passchendaele-1917.casualties: British-commanded armies: about 275,000 casualties; German casualties also heavy and disputed

battle-caporetto-1917.gloss: Austro-German forces broke the Italian line with infiltration tactics and gas. Caporetto caused a major Italian retreat and forced Allied support.

battle-megiddo-1918.gloss: Allenby's forces broke Ottoman defenses in Palestine through deception, cavalry exploitation, and air attacks. The victory led to the collapse of Ottoman control in Syria.

battle-amiens-1918.gloss: Allied troops used surprise, tanks, aircraft, and coordinated infantry to break German lines. Amiens opened the Hundred Days Offensive.

battle-shanghai-1937.gloss: China committed major forces to a brutal urban battle to resist Japan and draw world attention. Shanghai showed the scale of the war before World War II widened.

siege-warsaw-1939.gloss: Warsaw held out under bombing and siege during Germany's invasion of Poland. Its fall marked the collapse of organized Polish defense in the capital.

battle-dunkirk-1940.gloss: German forces trapped Allied armies near the coast, but a massive evacuation rescued much of the British Expeditionary Force. Dunkirk became both defeat and deliverance.

battle-moscow-1941.gloss: Soviet defenses and counterattacks stopped Germany's drive on Moscow. The battle ended the expectation of a quick German victory in the east.

battle-singapore-1942.gloss: Japanese forces captured Britain's major Southeast Asian base after a rapid Malayan campaign. The fall of Singapore was a severe shock to British imperial prestige.
battle-singapore-1942.casualties: Malaya and Singapore campaign: 8,708 Commonwealth killed or wounded and 130,000 captured; 9,824 Japanese casualties

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-bataan-1942.gloss: American and Filipino defenders held Bataan for months before surrendering. The battle delayed Japan but led to the Bataan Death March.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-midway-1942.gloss: American codebreaking and carrier aviation destroyed four Japanese carriers. Midway shifted initiative in the Pacific War toward the United States.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

guadalcanal-campaign-1942.gloss: Allied forces fought on land, sea, and air to hold Guadalcanal. The campaign became the first major Allied offensive victory in the Pacific.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-stalingrad-1942.gloss: Soviet forces encircled and destroyed the German Sixth Army after months of urban warfare. Stalingrad became the eastern front's decisive turning point.

battle-el-alamein-1942.gloss: Montgomery's army broke Axis positions in Egypt and forced a retreat across North Africa. El Alamein ended the Axis threat to the Suez Canal.

battle-kursk-1943.gloss: Germany's offensive against the Kursk salient failed against layered Soviet defenses. The battle confirmed Soviet strategic initiative on the eastern front.

battle-monte-cassino-1944.gloss: Allied forces fought through fortified mountain positions around the monastery of Monte Cassino. The victory opened the road toward Rome.

battle-normandy-1944.gloss: Allied landings and breakout operations opened a western front in Europe. Normandy made the liberation of France and the defeat of Germany a combined-front campaign.

battle-leyte-gulf-1944.gloss: Allied naval forces defeated Japan's attempt to disrupt the Philippines landings. Leyte Gulf is often considered the largest naval battle of World War II.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-iwo-jima-1945.gloss: US Marines captured a fortified volcanic island after intense fighting. The flag raising on Mount Suribachi became one of the war's most recognized images.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-okinawa-1945.gloss: Okinawa was the last major battle of the Pacific War and caused enormous military and civilian losses. It shaped planning for a possible invasion of Japan.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-berlin-1945.gloss: Soviet armies captured the German capital in the final European battle of World War II. Berlin's fall ended Nazi rule and divided the postwar city.

battle-bulge-1944.gloss: Germany launched a surprise winter offensive through the Ardennes. Allied resistance and counterattacks exhausted Germany's last major offensive in the west.

battle-manila-1945.gloss: Allied forces retook Manila in destructive urban combat. The battle devastated the city and caused catastrophic civilian losses.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-dien-bien-phu-1954.gloss: Viet Minh forces besieged and overran a French base in a remote valley. The defeat led to negotiations that ended French rule in Indochina.

battle-inchon-1950.gloss: MacArthur's amphibious landing at Inchon outflanked North Korean forces and recaptured Seoul. The operation reversed the early course of the Korean War.
battle-inchon-1950.casualties: United Nations Command: 224 killed and 809 wounded; North Korea: about 1,350 killed

battle-chosin-reservoir-1950.gloss: Chinese forces surrounded UN troops in freezing mountain terrain, but the UN column fought its way out. Chosin became a defining battle of endurance.
battle-chosin-reservoir-1950.casualties: United Nations Command: about 17,100 casualties including non-battle casualties; China: about 52,000 casualties including non-battle casualties

battle-hue-1968.gloss: Communist forces seized much of Hue during the Tet Offensive, leading to weeks of urban combat. The battle changed American perceptions of the war.
battle-hue-1968.casualties: US: about 250 killed and 1,554 wounded; North Vietnamese and Viet Cong: about 5,000 killed; civilian deaths also severe

battle-goose-green-1982.gloss: British paratroopers defeated Argentine forces at a key settlement after the San Carlos landings. Goose Green became one of the Falklands War's best-known ground battles.
battle-goose-green-1982.casualties: British: 18 killed and 64 wounded; Argentine: about 45-55 killed, 86 wounded, and 961 captured

battle-grozny-1994.gloss: Russian forces captured Grozny after costly urban fighting and severe destruction. The battle exposed weaknesses in Russian planning and the brutality of city warfare.

combatants: United States and Iraqi government forces
battle-fallujah-2004.gloss: Coalition forces retook Fallujah from insurgents in one of the Iraq War's fiercest urban battles. The operation became a reference point for modern city combat.
battle-fallujah-2004.casualties: Coalition: about 107 killed and 613 wounded; insurgents: roughly 1,200-2,000 killed; civilian deaths disputed

battle-mosul-2016.gloss: Iraqi forces and coalition partners retook Mosul after months of urban fighting. The victory ended Islamic State control of its largest Iraqi city.

battle-tuyuti-1866.title: Battle of Tuyutí
battle-tuyuti-1866.gloss: Paraguayan forces attacked the Triple Alliance camp but were repulsed with heavy losses. Tuyutí was one of the largest battles ever fought in South America.
battle-tuyuti-1866.casualties: Paraguay: 6,000 killed and 7,000 wounded; Triple Alliance: 996 killed and 3,232 wounded

battle-cuito-cuanavale-1987.outcome: Disputed tactical outcome; strategic pressure toward negotiated settlement
battle-cuito-cuanavale-1987.war: Angolan Civil War and South African Border War
battle-cuito-cuanavale-1987.gloss: FAPLA, Cuban, South African, and UNITA forces fought around Cuito Cuanavale in a prolonged Cold War battle. Its legacy is disputed, but it shaped negotiations over Angola and Namibia.

series.label: Angolan Civil War and South African Border War

battle-plataea-479bc.gloss: Greek hoplite armies defeated the Persian land force left in Greece after Salamis. Plataea ended Xerxes' invasion and confirmed Greek control of the mainland.

battle-hydaspes-326bc.war: Indian campaign of Alexander the Great
battle-hydaspes-326bc.gloss: Alexander defeated King Porus after a difficult river crossing and battle against elephants. Hydaspes pushed Macedonian campaigning to its eastern limit.

battle-chaeronea-338bc.gloss: Philip II defeated the allied Greek city-states and made Macedon the leading power in Greece. The battle set the stage for Alexander's later campaigns.

battle-issus-333bc.gloss: Alexander defeated Darius III in constricted terrain and captured the Persian royal camp. Issus proved that the Persian king himself could be beaten in battle.

battle-ipsus-301bc.gloss: The successors of Alexander defeated Antigonus, who died in battle. Ipsus fixed the division of Alexander's empire into durable Hellenistic kingdoms.

battle-metaurus-207bc.gloss: Roman forces defeated Hasdrubal Barca before he could join Hannibal in Italy. The victory removed Carthage's best chance to reinforce its Italian campaign.

battle-cynoscephalae-197bc.gloss: Roman legions defeated the Macedonian phalanx on broken ground. Cynoscephalae showed Rome's growing military influence in the Greek world.

battle-pydna-168bc.gloss: Rome defeated Perseus of Macedon and ended the Antigonid monarchy. Pydna completed Macedon's fall as an independent great power.

battle-milvian-bridge-312.war: Roman civil wars of the Tetrarchy
battle-milvian-bridge-312.gloss: Constantine defeated Maxentius outside Rome and became dominant in the western empire. The battle became famous for its association with Constantine's Christian vision.

battle-catalaunian-plains-451.gloss: A Roman-led coalition checked Attila's advance in Gaul. The battle showed the late empire relying on federate alliances against new imperial powers.

battle-badr-624.gloss: Muhammad's followers defeated a larger Meccan force in the first major battle of the early Muslim community. Badr became central to Islamic memory.

battle-nahavand-642.gloss: Arab Muslim forces defeated a major Sasanian army in western Iran. Nahavand accelerated the disintegration of Sasanian resistance.

battle-khartoum-1885.gloss: Mahdist forces captured Khartoum after a long siege and killed Charles Gordon. The fall shocked Britain and reshaped imperial policy in Sudan.

battle-covadonga-722.gloss: Asturian forces under Pelagius defeated a Muslim expedition in northern Iberia. Later tradition treated Covadonga as a symbolic beginning of the Reconquista.

battle-dorylaeum-1097.gloss: Crusader forces survived a Seljuk attack and restored their march across Anatolia. Dorylaeum showed both the danger and resilience of the First Crusade.

siege-jerusalem-1099.gloss: Crusader armies captured Jerusalem after a short siege and violent assault. The victory founded the Kingdom of Jerusalem and transformed crusading politics.

battle-legnica-1241.gloss: A Mongol army defeated Duke Henry II and his allies in Silesia. Legnica demonstrated the reach of Mongol campaigns into central Europe.

battle-stirling-bridge-1297.gloss: Scottish forces under William Wallace and Andrew Moray trapped the English at a narrow bridge. The victory revived Scottish resistance.

battle-bannockburn-1314.gloss: Robert Bruce defeated a larger English army near Stirling. Bannockburn secured Scottish momentum and became a national landmark.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-kosovo-1389.gloss: Ottoman and Serbian-led armies fought a costly battle on Kosovo Field. Its political consequences and memory shaped Balkan history for centuries.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-nicopolis-1396.gloss: Bayezid I defeated a large crusading army on the Danube. Nicopolis ended a major western attempt to halt Ottoman expansion in the Balkans.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-varna-1444.gloss: Ottoman forces defeated a crusader army and King Wladyslaw III was killed. Varna strengthened Ottoman control before the fall of Constantinople.

battle-castillon-1453.gloss: French artillery and field defenses destroyed an English attack. Castillon effectively ended the Hundred Years War in France.

battle-diu-1509.war: Portuguese expansion in the Indian Ocean

series.label: Portuguese expansion in the Indian Ocean

battle-diu-1509.gloss: A Portuguese fleet defeated a coalition near Diu and secured sea power in the western Indian Ocean. The battle reshaped Asian maritime trade routes.

battle-cajamarca-1532.war: Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire

series.label: Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire

battle-cajamarca-1532.gloss: Pizarro captured Atahualpa in a sudden attack at Cajamarca. The ambush destabilized the Inca Empire and opened the conquest of Peru.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

siege-malta-1565.gloss: Ottoman forces failed to take Malta after a hard siege. The defense became a major symbol of Mediterranean resistance to Ottoman expansion.

spanish-armada-1588.gloss: England disrupted Spain's invasion fleet through fireships, weather, and naval pressure. The Armada campaign became central to English maritime memory.

battle-white-mountain-1620.gloss: Imperial and Catholic League forces crushed the Bohemian revolt near Prague. White Mountain reshaped Bohemia and widened the Thirty Years War.

battle-naseby-1645.gloss: The New Model Army defeated Charles I's main field army. Naseby made Parliament's victory in the first civil war likely.

battle-dunbar-1650.gloss: Cromwell defeated a larger Scottish army after maneuvering out of a dangerous position. Dunbar opened Scotland to English occupation.

battle-poltava-1709.gloss: Peter the Great defeated Charles XII and broke Swedish offensive power. Poltava marked Russia's rise as a major European military state.

battle-quebec-1759.gloss: British troops scaled the heights near Quebec and defeated Montcalm on the Plains of Abraham. The victory helped decide control of New France.

battle-buxar-1764.war: British East India Company expansion in India

series.label: British East India Company expansion in India

battle-buxar-1764.gloss: Company forces defeated the Nawab of Awadh, the Nawab of Bengal, and the Mughal emperor. Buxar consolidated British revenue power in Bengal.

battle-saratoga-1777.gloss: American forces forced Burgoyne's army to surrender in upstate New York. Saratoga helped bring France openly into the war.

battle-culloden-1746.gloss: Government forces defeated the Jacobite army in a short, destructive battle. Culloden ended the last major Jacobite attempt to restore the Stuart line.

battle-marengo-1800.gloss: Napoleon recovered from near defeat and beat the Austrians in northern Italy. Marengo secured his political standing as First Consul.

battle-jena-auerstedt-1806.gloss: French forces defeated Prussia in twin battles on the same day. Jena-Auerstedt shattered the old Prussian army and transformed German politics.

battle-friedland-1807.gloss: Napoleon defeated Bennigsen's Russian army near Friedland. The victory led to the Treaties of Tilsit and a temporary Franco-Russian settlement.

battle-new-orleans-1815.gloss: Andrew Jackson's forces repelled a British attack after the peace treaty had been signed but before news arrived. The victory boosted American national confidence.

battle-chacabuco-1817.gloss: San Martin's Army of the Andes defeated royalists after crossing from Argentina. Chacabuco opened the road to Santiago.

battle-maipu-1818.gloss: Patriot forces defeated a royalist army near Santiago. Maipu secured Chilean independence after the crisis following Cancha Rayada.

battle-boyaca-1819.gloss: Bolivar's army defeated royalist forces and captured the road to Bogota. Boyaca secured New Granada for the independence movement.

battle-carabobo-1821.gloss: Bolivar's forces defeated the main royalist army in Venezuela. Carabobo made Spanish control of the region untenable.

battle-puebla-1862.gloss: Mexican forces repelled a French assault on Puebla. The victory became a symbol of resistance celebrated as Cinco de Mayo.

battle-chapultepec-1847.gloss: US forces stormed Chapultepec Castle during the campaign for Mexico City. The battle opened the way to the capital.

combatants: British Empire, France, and Ottoman Empire
battle-balaclava-1854.gloss: Allied forces held the port of Balaclava against Russian attack. The battle is remembered for the Charge of the Light Brigade.

battle-sedan-1870.gloss: German forces encircled and captured Napoleon III's army. Sedan collapsed the Second Empire and transformed the war.

battle-little-bighorn-1876.gloss: Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho warriors destroyed Custer's battalion. The victory became iconic but brought intensified US campaigns.
battle-little-bighorn-1876.casualties: Lakota, Northern Cheyenne, and Arapaho: about 31-136 killed; United States: 268 dead and 55 wounded

battle-rorkes-drift-1879.gloss: A small British garrison held a mission station against repeated Zulu attacks. Rorke's Drift became famous for its defense after Isandlwana.

battle-blood-river-1838.gloss: Voortrekker commandos defeated a Zulu army from a fortified wagon laager. The battle became central to Afrikaner nationalist memory.

battle-manila-bay-1898.gloss: Dewey's squadron destroyed the Spanish fleet in Manila Bay. The victory brought the United States into Philippine imperial politics.

battle-san-juan-hill-1898.gloss: US and Cuban forces captured heights outside Santiago de Cuba. The battle became famous through the Rough Riders and Theodore Roosevelt.

battle-spion-kop-1900.gloss: Boer forces repelled a British attempt to seize a key hill during the relief of Ladysmith. Spion Kop exposed British tactical failures.

battle-tanga-1914.gloss: German colonial forces defeated a larger British landing force at Tanga. The battle shaped the early East African campaign.

battle-jutland-1916.outcome: British strategic advantage; German tactical survival
battle-jutland-1916.gloss: The main British and German battle fleets clashed in the North Sea. Jutland was indecisive tactically but left British naval blockade strategy intact.

battle-cambrai-1917.gloss: British forces used tanks in a large coordinated attack against the Hindenburg Line. Cambrai showed both promise and limits of armored breakthrough.

battle-belleau-wood-1918.gloss: US Marines and Army troops fought German forces in close terrain near the Marne. Belleau Wood became a defining American memory of World War I.

battle-warsaw-1920.gloss: Polish forces counterattacked and stopped the Soviet advance on Warsaw. The victory preserved Polish independence and checked westward Bolshevik momentum.

battle-khalkhin-gol-1939.gloss: Zhukov defeated Japanese forces on the Mongolian frontier with armor, artillery, and air power. The defeat influenced Japan's strategic turn southward.

battle-britain-1940.gloss: The Royal Air Force defeated Germany's attempt to gain air superiority over Britain. The battle prevented invasion and made air defense decisive.

attack-pearl-harbor-1941.outcome: Japanese tactical victory; American entry into war

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

attack-pearl-harbor-1941.gloss: Japanese aircraft struck the US Pacific Fleet at Pearl Harbor. The attack brought the United States into World War II.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-coral-sea-1942.gloss: Carrier aircraft fought without opposing surface fleets sighting each other. Coral Sea checked Japan's move toward Port Moresby.

battle-kasserine-pass-1943.gloss: German and Italian forces mauled inexperienced American units in Tunisia. Kasserine forced rapid Allied changes in command and tactics.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-tarawa-1943.gloss: US Marines captured Betio after a costly amphibious assault. Tarawa taught hard lessons for later Pacific landings.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-saipan-1944.gloss: US forces captured Saipan, putting Japan within range of B-29 bombers. The defeat triggered a political crisis in Tokyo.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-peleliu-1944.gloss: US Marines and Army troops fought entrenched Japanese defenders in coral ridges and caves. Peleliu became controversial for its cost and limited strategic value.

operation-market-garden-1944.gloss: Allied airborne and ground forces tried to seize bridges into Germany. Market Garden failed at Arnhem and left the war in the west unresolved.

battle-hurtgen-forest-1944.gloss: American forces fought through dense forest, mines, and fortifications near the German border. Hurtgen became one of the US Army's costliest campaigns in Europe.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-imphal-1944.gloss: Allied forces defeated a Japanese invasion attempt in northeastern India. Imphal was a turning point in the Burma campaign.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-kohima-1944.gloss: Fighting around Kohima Ridge stopped Japan's advance toward India. The battle became famous for close combat around the tennis court.

battle-crete-1941.gloss: German paratroopers captured Crete after heavy losses. The operation proved airborne forces could seize an island but at a severe cost.

battle-kiev-1941.gloss: German forces encircled huge Soviet armies around Kyiv. The victory was one of the largest encirclements of the war but delayed the drive on Moscow.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-guam-1944.gloss: US forces recaptured Guam after weeks of fighting. The island became a major base for operations against Japan.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-wake-island-1941.gloss: American defenders repelled the first Japanese landing attempt before surrendering to a second attack. Wake became an early Pacific War symbol of resistance.

battle-yalu-river-1950.gloss: Chinese forces launched a major counteroffensive against UN armies in North Korea. The battle drove UN forces south and transformed the Korean War.

battle-khe-sanh-1968.outcome: American and South Vietnamese defensive victory
battle-khe-sanh-1968.gloss: North Vietnamese forces besieged the US Marine base at Khe Sanh during the Tet period. The battle became a test of firepower and logistics.

battle-ia-drang-1965.gloss: US air cavalry fought North Vietnamese regulars in the first major US-NVA battle. Ia Drang previewed the war's reliance on helicopters and firepower.

battle-longewala-1971.gloss: A small Indian post held against a Pakistani armored attack until air support arrived. Longewala became a famous Indian defensive victory.

battle-73-easting-1991.gloss: Coalition armored units destroyed Iraqi Republican Guard elements in the desert. 73 Easting became a model case of modern armored warfare.

battle-mogadishu-1993.outcome: Somali tactical success; US withdrawal pressure
battle-mogadishu-1993.gloss: A US raid turned into a prolonged urban battle after helicopters were shot down. Mogadishu changed American and UN policy in Somalia.

battle-tora-bora-2001.gloss: US and Afghan forces attacked al-Qaeda positions in the mountains near Tora Bora. The battle became controversial because Osama bin Laden escaped.

battle-marawi-2017.title: Siege of Marawi
battle-marawi-2017.gloss: Philippine forces retook Marawi after months of urban combat against militants. The battle exposed the danger of Islamic State-linked insurgency in Southeast Asia.

battle-raqqa-2017.gloss: US-backed Syrian Democratic Forces captured Islamic State's de facto capital after a destructive urban battle. Raqqa marked a major defeat for the caliphate project.

siege-kobani-2014.gloss: Kurdish defenders held Kobani against Islamic State assault with coalition air support. The siege became a symbolic turning point in the fight against ISIS.

battle-basra-2003.gloss: British forces captured Basra during the invasion of Iraq after fighting around the city and airport. Control of Basra secured a key southern port and oil region.

battle-algiers-1956.gloss: French forces dismantled FLN networks in Algiers through raids, intelligence, and torture. The battle won the city tactically but damaged France politically.

battle-el-gueittar-1943.gloss: US forces repelled German armored attacks in Tunisia after the lessons of Kasserine. El Guettar showed improving American battlefield performance.

battle-river-plate-1939.location: South Atlantic near Uruguay and Argentina

series.label: World War II Atlantic naval war

battle-river-plate-1939.gloss: British cruisers forced the German raider Admiral Graf Spee into Montevideo, where it was scuttled. The battle was an early Allied naval success.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-milne-bay-1942.gloss: Australian and American forces defeated a Japanese landing at Milne Bay. It was one of the first clear Allied land victories against Japanese troops.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

kokoda-track-campaign-1942.gloss: Australian forces fought a difficult jungle campaign to stop Japan's advance toward Port Moresby. Kokoda became central to Australian wartime memory.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-savo-island-1942.gloss: Japanese cruisers surprised and defeated Allied warships near Guadalcanal. Savo Island was one of the US Navy's worst surface defeats.

battle-lae-1943.location: Lae and Salamaua, Papua New Guinea

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-lae-1943.gloss: Allied operations captured Salamaua and Lae through coordinated landings and overland advances. The campaign pushed Japan back in New Guinea.

battle-buna-gona-1942.location: Buna and Gona, Papua New Guinea

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-buna-gona-1942.gloss: Australian and American troops fought entrenched Japanese positions on the north coast of New Guinea. Buna-Gona was a costly Allied victory.

battle-riachuelo-1865.gloss: Brazilian warships defeated Paraguay on the Parana River. Riachuelo secured Allied river control during the Paraguayan War.
battle-riachuelo-1865.casualties: Paraguay: about 750 casualties; Brazil: 104 killed, 123 wounded, and 20 missing

battle-kambula-1879.gloss: British forces defeated Zulu attacks from a prepared defensive position. Kambula helped restore British confidence after Isandlwana.

battle-majuba-hill-1881.gloss: Boer marksmen defeated British troops on Majuba Hill. The battle helped force a settlement recognizing Transvaal self-government.

battle-ohaeawai-1845.gloss: British troops failed against a fortified Maori pa at Ohaeawai. The battle demonstrated the sophistication of Maori defensive engineering.

battle-gate-pa-1864.gloss: Maori defenders at Gate Pa repelled a much larger British assault. The battle became a notable defeat for imperial forces in New Zealand.

battle-fei-river-383.gloss: Eastern Jin forces routed a much larger Former Qin army. The defeat fractured Former Qin power and preserved southern Chinese independence.

battle-siffin-657.gloss: The armies of Ali and Muawiya fought during the first major Islamic civil war. Siffin deepened the political and religious divisions of the early caliphate.

battle-guadalete-711.gloss: Umayyad-led forces defeated the Visigothic army of King Roderic. The battle opened the rapid Muslim conquest of most of Iberia.

battle-stamford-bridge-1066.gloss: Harold Godwinson defeated Harald Hardrada and Tostig Godwinson in northern England. The victory ended the Norwegian invasion but left England exposed before Hastings.

battle-myriokephalon-1176.gloss: Seljuk forces ambushed a Byzantine army in Anatolia. The battle damaged Byzantine hopes of restoring control over the interior of Asia Minor.

battle-arsuf-1191.gloss: Richard I defeated Saladin near Arsuf during the Third Crusade. The victory restored Crusader morale and secured the coastal march toward Jaffa.

battle-las-navas-1212.location: Near Las Navas de Tolosa, Spain
battle-las-navas-1212.gloss: A coalition of Iberian kingdoms defeated the Almohad army in Andalusia. The battle shifted the long-term balance of power in the Reconquista.

battle-crecy-1346.gloss: English longbowmen and defensive positioning defeated repeated French attacks. Crecy became a landmark battle in the Hundred Years War.

battle-poitiers-1356.gloss: Edward the Black Prince defeated a larger French army and captured King John II. Poitiers intensified the political crisis in France.

battle-lake-poyang-1363.gloss: Zhu Yuanzhang defeated Chen Youliang in a vast naval battle. The victory cleared Zhu path toward founding the Ming dynasty.

battle-ankara-1402.gloss: Timur defeated and captured Ottoman sultan Bayezid I. Ankara plunged the Ottoman state into an interregnum and delayed its expansion.

battle-lutzen-1632.gloss: Swedish forces defeated an Imperial army, but Gustavus Adolphus was killed. The battle preserved Swedish influence while changing the war leadership.

battle-nordlingen-1634.gloss: Imperial and Spanish forces crushed the Protestant field army. Nordlingen reversed Swedish momentum and pushed France toward direct intervention.

battle-fontenoy-1745.gloss: French forces defeated an Allied army in the Austrian Netherlands. Fontenoy became one of France major eighteenth-century battlefield victories.

battle-leuthen-1757.gloss: Frederick the Great used an oblique attack to defeat a larger Austrian army. Leuthen is often treated as one of his finest victories.

battle-wandiwash-1760.gloss: British forces defeated the French in southern India. Wandiwash helped end French hopes of matching British power in India.

battle-panipat-1761.gloss: Ahmad Shah Durrani defeated the Maratha Confederacy in a huge and costly battle. Panipat reshaped power politics across northern India.

battle-assaye-1803.gloss: Arthur Wellesley defeated a larger Maratha army in western India. Assaye strengthened Company control and Wellesley reputation.

battle-shiloh-1862.gloss: Union forces survived a surprise Confederate attack and counterattacked the next day. Shiloh revealed how costly the Civil War would become.

battle-antietam-1862.gloss: Antietam halted Lee invasion of Maryland after the bloodiest single day of the war. The result gave Lincoln space to issue the Emancipation Proclamation.

second-marne-1918.gloss: Allied forces stopped Germany final major offensive and began the counteroffensive phase of 1918. The battle marked a decisive turn toward Allied victory.

battle-nanking-1937.gloss: Japanese forces captured the Chinese capital after hard fighting. The battle was followed by the Nanjing Massacre and became central to wartime memory.

battle-wuhan-1938.gloss: Chinese forces fought a long defense around Wuhan before withdrawing. The battle slowed Japan and moved the war into a prolonged phase.

battle-suomussalmi-1939.gloss: Finnish troops destroyed Soviet columns in deep winter conditions. Suomussalmi became a classic example of mobility, terrain, and small-unit tactics.

siege-leningrad-1941.gloss: Axis forces encircled Leningrad for nearly 900 days, causing mass civilian starvation. The city endured until Soviet offensives broke the siege.

battle-brody-1941.gloss: Soviet mechanized corps counterattacked German armor in one of the largest tank battles of 1941. The defeat exposed early Soviet command and coordination problems.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-bismarck-sea-1943.gloss: Allied aircraft destroyed a Japanese convoy bound for New Guinea. The battle sharply limited Japan ability to reinforce the theater.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-philippine-sea-1944.gloss: US carrier forces defeated a major Japanese fleet sortie near the Marianas. The battle crippled Japanese naval aviation.

battle-seelow-heights-1945.gloss: Soviet armies broke through the German defenses east of Berlin. Seelow Heights opened the final assault on the German capital.

battle-long-tan-1966.gloss: Australian and New Zealand troops fought a much larger force in a rubber plantation. Long Tan became one of Australia best-known Vietnam War battles.

battle-maling-342bc.gloss: Qi forces used deception and ambush to defeat Wei. Maling became a classic Chinese example of stratagem and battlefield intelligence.

battle-leuctra-371bc.gloss: Theban forces under Epaminondas broke Spartan battlefield dominance. Leuctra reshaped Greek politics and military tactics.

battle-mantinea-362bc.gloss: Epaminondas won but was killed, leaving no clear hegemon in Greece. The battle ended the brief peak of Theban power.

battle-granicus-334bc.gloss: Alexander crossed into Asia and defeated Persian satrapal forces at the Granicus. The victory opened the Achaemenid Empire to his campaign.

battle-trebia-218bc.gloss: Hannibal lured Roman forces into a cold river crossing and enveloped them. Trebia confirmed the danger of his Italian invasion.

battle-lake-trasimene-217bc.gloss: Hannibal ambushed a Roman army beside Lake Trasimene. It became one of the largest successful ambushes in ancient warfare.

battle-changping-260bc.gloss: Qin defeated Zhao in a catastrophic campaign that broke a major rival. Changping accelerated Qin movement toward unifying China.

battle-guandu-200.war: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty

series.label: Wars at the end of the Han dynasty

battle-guandu-200.gloss: Cao Cao defeated Yuan Shao despite being outnumbered. Guandu set the balance of power in northern China before the Three Kingdoms.

battle-edessa-260.gloss: Shapur I defeated the Romans and captured Emperor Valerian. Edessa was one of Rome most humiliating imperial defeats.

battle-naissus-269.war: Gothic invasions of the Roman Empire
battle-naissus-269.gloss: Roman forces crushed a major Gothic invasion in the Balkans. The victory helped stabilize the empire during a period of severe crisis.

battle-strasbourg-357.gloss: Julian defeated a larger Alamannic force near Strasbourg. The battle restored Roman authority along part of the Rhine frontier.

battle-dara-530.gloss: Belisarius used fieldworks and maneuver to defeat a Sasanian army. Dara became one of the best-known early Byzantine victories.

battle-hulao-621.gloss: Li Shimin defeated rival forces at Hulao Pass. The battle was decisive in the Tang dynasty consolidation of China.

battle-ajnadayn-634.gloss: Rashidun forces defeated a Byzantine field army in Palestine. Ajnadayn opened the way for further Muslim advances in the Levant.

battle-kleidion-1014.gloss: Basil II defeated the Bulgarian army at Kleidion. The battle helped bring Bulgaria under Byzantine control.

siege-antioch-1097.gloss: Crusaders endured a long siege and then a counter-siege at Antioch. The victory kept the First Crusade alive on the road to Jerusalem.

battle-ascalon-1099.gloss: Crusader forces defeated a Fatimid army soon after the capture of Jerusalem. Ascalon secured the first wave of crusader conquests.

battle-montgisard-1177.gloss: A small Crusader army under Baldwin IV surprised and defeated Saladin. Montgisard temporarily checked Ayyubid pressure on Jerusalem.

siege-acre-1189.gloss: The long siege of Acre became the central operation of the Third Crusade. Its capture gave the Crusaders a coastal base.

battle-bouvines-1214.gloss: Philip II defeated a coalition of enemies at Bouvines. The victory strengthened Capetian monarchy and weakened King John position.

siege-baghdad-1258.war: Mongol conquest of the Abbasid Caliphate
siege-baghdad-1258.gloss: Mongol forces captured Baghdad and ended the Abbasid caliphate political power. The siege became a symbol of catastrophic urban destruction.

battle-golden-spurs-1302.gloss: Flemish infantry defeated French cavalry near Kortrijk. The battle challenged assumptions about knightly battlefield dominance.

battle-sluys-1340.gloss: English ships destroyed much of the French fleet at Sluys. The naval victory gave England control of Channel movement early in the war.

battle-kulikovo-1380.gloss: Dmitry Donskoy defeated Mamai forces at Kulikovo. The battle became central to Russian memory of resistance to Horde power.

siege-orleans-1429.gloss: French forces relieved Orleans after Joan of Arc arrival. The victory reversed momentum in the later Hundred Years War.

battle-bosworth-field-1485.gloss: Henry Tudor defeated Richard III at Bosworth Field. The battle ended Plantagenet rule and began the Tudor dynasty.

battle-flodden-1513.gloss: English forces defeated the Scots and killed King James IV. Flodden was one of Scotland most severe military disasters.

battle-khanwa-1527.gloss: Babur defeated Rana Sanga coalition after Panipat. Khanwa helped secure Mughal power in northern India.

battle-kawanakajima-1561.gloss: The fourth battle of Kawanakajima became the most famous clash between Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin. Its drama made it a fixture of Sengoku memory.

battle-talikota-1565.gloss: The Deccan sultanates defeated Vijayanagara and devastated its capital region. Talikota marked a major shift in South Indian power.

battle-haldighati-1576.gloss: Mughal forces fought Rana Pratap army in the Aravalli hills. The battle became a powerful symbol of Rajput resistance.

battle-myeongnyang-1597.gloss: Admiral Yi Sun-sin defeated a much larger Japanese fleet in a narrow strait. Myeongnyang is one of Korea most celebrated naval victories.

battle-noryang-1598.gloss: Allied Korean and Ming forces struck the retreating Japanese fleet. Noryang ended the naval phase of the Imjin War and cost Yi Sun-sin his life.

battle-edgehill-1642.gloss: The first major pitched battle of the English Civil War failed to decide the conflict. Edgehill showed that the war would not end quickly.

battle-ramillies-1706.gloss: Marlborough defeated the French army in the Spanish Netherlands. Ramillies opened much of the region to Allied occupation.

battle-malplaquet-1709.gloss: The Allies forced the French from the field but suffered heavy losses. Malplaquet showed the growing cost of the war.

battle-rossbach-1757.gloss: Frederick the Great crushed a larger Franco-Imperial army by maneuver. Rossbach became one of Prussia most famous victories.

battle-kunersdorf-1759.gloss: Russian and Austrian forces nearly destroyed Frederick army. Kunersdorf brought Prussia close to disaster.

battle-bunker-hill-1775.gloss: British troops took the heights near Boston but suffered heavy losses. Bunker Hill proved the colonial forces would fight hard.

battle-trenton-1776.gloss: Washington crossed the Delaware and surprised the Hessian garrison at Trenton. The victory revived American morale after a difficult campaign.

battle-cowpens-1781.gloss: Daniel Morgan used militia and regulars in a layered defense to defeat Banastre Tarleton. Cowpens damaged British strength in the southern campaign.

battle-guilford-court-house-1781.gloss: Cornwallis won the field but took heavy losses. Guilford Court House weakened the British southern army before Yorktown.

battle-eylau-1807.gloss: French and Russian armies fought a brutal winter battle with no decisive result. Eylau challenged Napoleon image of effortless victory.

battle-aspern-essling-1809.gloss: Archduke Charles defeated Napoleon after the French crossed the Danube. Aspern-Essling was Napoleon first major battlefield defeat.

battle-wagram-1809.gloss: Napoleon defeated Austria in a massive battle near Vienna. Wagram ended the War of the Fifth Coalition.

battle-salamanca-1812.gloss: Wellington exploited a French deployment error and won a major victory. Salamanca opened the road toward Madrid.

battle-ligny-1815.gloss: Napoleon defeated Blucher Prussian army two days before Waterloo. The victory was not decisive enough to prevent Prussian intervention later.

battle-quatre-bras-1815.gloss: Wellington blocked Ney at Quatre Bras while the Prussians fought at Ligny. The battle shaped the movements before Waterloo.

battle-navarino-1827.gloss: Allied fleets destroyed the Ottoman-Egyptian fleet at Navarino. The naval victory helped secure Greek independence.

battle-first-bull-run-1861.gloss: The first major battle of the Civil War ended in a Confederate victory. Bull Run shattered expectations of a short war.

battle-fredericksburg-1862.gloss: Union assaults failed against strong Confederate positions on Marye Heights. Fredericksburg became a symbol of costly frontal attack.

battle-chancellorsville-1863.gloss: Lee divided his army and defeated a larger Union force. Chancellorsville was his greatest victory but cost Stonewall Jackson.

battle-chickamauga-1863.gloss: Confederate forces routed much of the Union army in northern Georgia. Chickamauga was the Confederacy major western victory.

battle-atlanta-1864.gloss: Union forces defeated Confederate attacks east of Atlanta during Sherman campaign. The fall of Atlanta helped secure Lincoln reelection.

siege-plevna-1877.gloss: Ottoman defenses at Plevna delayed the Russian advance for months. The siege demonstrated the power of modern entrenched firepower.

battle-ulundi-1879.gloss: British forces defeated the Zulu army near the royal kraal at Ulundi. The battle effectively ended organized Zulu resistance in the war.

battle-mons-1914.gloss: The British Expeditionary Force fought its first major battle against Germany at Mons. The battle began the long British retreat from Belgium.

first-ypres-1914.gloss: Allied forces stopped German attempts to break through around Ypres. The battle helped establish the trench line of the Western Front.

second-ypres-1915.gloss: German forces used poison gas on a large scale near Ypres. The battle introduced a terrifying new weapon to the Western Front.

gorlice-tarnow-offensive-1915.gloss: German and Austro-Hungarian forces broke through Russian lines in Galicia. Gorlice-Tarnow forced a major Russian retreat.

brusilov-offensive-1916.gloss: Brusilov offensive shattered Austro-Hungarian armies and drew in German reserves. It was Russia most successful operation of the war.

battle-france-1940.gloss: Germany defeated France and the Allied armies in a rapid campaign. The battle transformed the strategic situation in Europe.

battle-narvik-1940.outcome: Allied local victory; German campaign success
battle-narvik-1940.gloss: Naval and land battles around Narvik contested control of northern Norway. Allied forces won locally but withdrew after the wider German campaign succeeded.

siege-tobruk-1941.gloss: Allied defenders held Tobruk against Axis siege for months. The defense disrupted Axis operations in North Africa.

battle-gazala-1942.gloss: Rommel defeated the Eighth Army around Gazala and captured Tobruk soon after. The battle opened the Axis advance toward Egypt.

battle-changsha-1939.gloss: Chinese forces repelled a Japanese attempt to take Changsha. The victory demonstrated that Japan could still be stopped in major inland operations.

battle-changde-1943.gloss: Chinese troops fought a costly defense of Changde against Japanese forces. The battle became one of the major Chinese urban fights of the war.

battle-anzio-1944.gloss: Allied forces landed at Anzio to outflank German defenses in Italy but became pinned near the beachhead. The battle ended with the breakout toward Rome.

operation-bagration-1944.gloss: Soviet armies destroyed German Army Group Centre in a massive offensive. Bagration was one of Germany greatest defeats of the war.

falaise-pocket-1944.gloss: Allied armies encircled large German forces during the breakout from Normandy. The Falaise pocket helped end the German position in France.

battle-aegospotami-405bc.gloss: Spartan forces destroyed the Athenian fleet at Aegospotami. The battle cut Athens off from grain supplies and led directly to its surrender.

battle-amphipolis-422bc.gloss: Brasidas defeated an Athenian force near Amphipolis, and both Brasidas and Cleon were killed. The battle helped create the conditions for the Peace of Nicias.

battle-raphia-217bc.gloss: Ptolemy IV defeated Antiochus III in one of the largest battles of the Hellenistic age. Raphia preserved Ptolemaic control of Coele-Syria for a time.

battle-magnesia-190bc.gloss: Roman and allied forces defeated Antiochus III at Magnesia. The battle confirmed Roman power in the eastern Mediterranean.

battle-aquae-sextiae-102bc.gloss: Gaius Marius defeated the Teutones and Ambrones in southern Gaul. The victory helped end the immediate threat of large northern migrations into Roman territory.

battle-vercellae-101bc.gloss: Roman forces under Marius and Catulus destroyed the Cimbri at Vercellae. The battle ended one of the gravest threats Rome had faced since Hannibal.

battle-pharsalus-48bc.gloss: Caesar defeated Pompey in Greece despite being outnumbered. Pharsalus made Caesar the dominant figure in the Roman civil war.

battle-munda-45bc.gloss: Caesar defeated the last major Pompeian army at Munda. The battle ended organized resistance to his rule in the Roman world.

battle-philippi-42bc.gloss: Antony and Octavian defeated Brutus and Cassius at Philippi. The battle ended the republican faction that had killed Caesar.

battle-salsu-612.gloss: Goguryeo forces destroyed a huge Sui army during its retreat. Salsu became one of Korea most famous victories and weakened the Sui dynasty.

battle-nineveh-627.gloss: Heraclius defeated the Sasanian army near Nineveh after a bold winter campaign. The victory helped force Persia to make peace with Byzantium.

battle-fahl-635.gloss: Rashidun forces defeated Byzantine troops near Fahl after earlier victories in Palestine. The battle further weakened Byzantine control in the southern Levant.

siege-jerusalem-637.gloss: Jerusalem surrendered to Rashidun forces after a siege during the Muslim conquest of the Levant. The city transfer became one of the most important events of the early Islamic conquests.

combatants: Forces of Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr
battle-camel-656.gloss: Ali forces defeated opponents led by Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr near Basra. The battle opened the first major civil war within the Muslim community.

battle-karbala-680.gloss: Umayyad forces killed Husayn ibn Ali and his small party at Karbala. The battle became central to Shia memory and Islamic history.

siege-constantinople-717.gloss: Byzantine defenders held Constantinople against a major Umayyad siege by land and sea. The victory preserved Byzantium and checked Umayyad expansion toward southeastern Europe.

battle-dandanaqan-1040.gloss: Seljuk forces defeated the Ghaznavids near Merv. Dandanaqan opened the way for Seljuk dominance across Iran and the wider Middle East.

battle-civitate-1053.gloss: Norman forces defeated a papal-led army at Civitate and captured Pope Leo IX. The battle confirmed Norman power in southern Italy.

battle-didgori-1121.gloss: David IV of Georgia defeated a much larger Seljuk-led coalition at Didgori. The victory helped launch the Georgian Golden Age.

battle-alarcos-1195.gloss: Almohad forces defeated Castile at Alarcos. The defeat delayed Castilian expansion until the Christian coalition victory at Las Navas de Tolosa.

battle-kose-dag-1243.gloss: Mongol forces defeated the Sultanate of Rum at Kose Dag. The battle reduced Seljuk power in Anatolia and reshaped the region political future.

battle-la-forbie-1244.gloss: Crusader and allied Syrian forces were crushed near Gaza at La Forbie. The defeat badly weakened the military strength of the Crusader states.

battle-mansurah-1250.gloss: Egyptian forces defeated the crusading army of Louis IX around Mansurah. The battle led to the capture of the French king and the failure of the Seventh Crusade.

battle-worringen-1288.gloss: Brabant and its allies defeated Guelders in the War of the Limburg Succession. Worringen shaped power in the lower Rhine and Low Countries.

battle-halidon-hill-1333.gloss: English forces defeated a Scottish army attacking uphill at Halidon Hill. The battle showed the deadly combination of archers and defensive ground.

battle-nevilles-cross-1346.gloss: English forces defeated and captured King David II of Scotland near Durham. The battle weakened Scotland during a critical phase of the Hundred Years War.

battle-formigny-1450.gloss: French forces defeated an English army in Normandy at Formigny. The battle helped end English control of Normandy.

battle-tewkesbury-1471.gloss: Yorkist forces defeated the Lancastrians at Tewkesbury and killed or captured much of their leadership. The battle secured Edward IV return to power.

battle-chaldiran-1514.gloss: Ottoman forces defeated the Safavids with artillery and firearms at Chaldiran. The battle shaped the Ottoman-Safavid frontier and sectarian politics of the region.

battle-marj-dabiq-1516.gloss: Selim I defeated the Mamluks at Marj Dabiq, opening Syria to Ottoman conquest. The battle began the collapse of Mamluk power.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

siege-rhodes-1522.gloss: Ottoman forces captured Rhodes after a major siege against the Knights Hospitaller. The victory removed a key Christian naval base in the eastern Mediterranean.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-preveza-1538.gloss: Barbarossa defeated a Holy League fleet near Preveza. The battle confirmed Ottoman naval strength in the eastern Mediterranean before Lepanto.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

siege-szigetvar-1566.gloss: Suleiman forces captured Szigetvar after a fierce defense by Nikola Zrinski. The siege delayed the Ottoman advance and became legendary in Hungarian and Croatian memory.

battle-ivry-1590.gloss: Henry IV defeated the Catholic League at Ivry. The victory strengthened his claim during the French Wars of Religion.

battle-kinsale-1601.gloss: English forces defeated Irish and Spanish allies at Kinsale. The battle broke the main Gaelic resistance to Tudor conquest in Ireland.

battle-marston-moor-1644.gloss: Parliamentarian and Scottish Covenanter forces defeated the Royalists at Marston Moor. The battle secured northern England for Parliament.

battle-worcester-1651.gloss: Cromwell defeated Charles II royalist army at Worcester. The battle ended the main military phase of the Wars of the Three Kingdoms.

four-days-battle-1666.gloss: Dutch and English fleets fought for four days in one of the longest naval battles of the age of sail. The Dutch victory showed the continuing strength of their fleet.

battle-boyne-1690.gloss: William III defeated James II forces at the Boyne. The battle became a defining event in Irish and British political memory.

series.label: Ottoman expansion in Europe and the Mediterranean

battle-zenta-1697.gloss: Prince Eugene surprised and destroyed an Ottoman army crossing the Tisza at Zenta. The victory helped force the Treaty of Karlowitz.

battle-oudenarde-1708.gloss: Marlborough and Eugene defeated a French army at Oudenarde. The victory opened the way for the Allied siege of Lille.

battle-minden-1759.gloss: An Allied army defeated French forces at Minden during the Seven Years War. The battle checked French power in western Germany.

battle-porto-novo-1781.gloss: Company forces under Eyre Coote defeated Hyder Ali at Porto Novo. The battle checked Mysore momentum in the Second Anglo-Mysore War.

battle-valmy-1792.gloss: French forces stopped the Prussian advance at Valmy. The battle helped save the Revolution and gave confidence to the new French Republic.

battle-bailen-1808.gloss: Spanish forces forced a French corps to surrender at Bailen. The defeat shattered Napoleon aura of invincibility in Spain.

battle-vitoria-1813.gloss: Wellington defeated Joseph Bonaparte army at Vitoria. The battle broke French power in Spain and pushed the war toward the Pyrenees.

battle-bladensburg-1814.gloss: British troops routed American defenders at Bladensburg. The victory opened the way to Washington, D.C., where British forces burned public buildings.

battle-horseshoe-bend-1814.gloss: Andrew Jackson forces defeated the Red Stick Creeks at Horseshoe Bend. The battle ended the Creek War and forced major land cessions.
battle-horseshoe-bend-1814.casualties: United States and allies: fewer than 50 killed and 154 wounded; Red Stick Creeks: roughly 800 killed

battle-alamo-1836.gloss: Mexican forces stormed the Alamo after a thirteen-day siege. The defeat became a rallying symbol for Texian independence forces.

battle-san-jacinto-1836.gloss: Sam Houston army surprised and defeated Santa Anna at San Jacinto. The battle effectively secured Texas independence from Mexico.

battle-cerro-gordo-1847.gloss: Winfield Scott defeated Santa Anna at Cerro Gordo during the advance from Veracruz. The victory opened the route toward Mexico City.

battle-inkerman-1854.gloss: Allied troops defeated a Russian attack near Inkerman in confused foggy fighting. The battle helped secure the siege lines around Sevastopol.

battle-wilderness-1864.gloss: Grant and Lee fought in dense Virginia woodland at the Wilderness. The battle began the relentless Overland Campaign toward Richmond and Petersburg.

battle-cold-harbor-1864.gloss: Union assaults failed against entrenched Confederate positions at Cold Harbor. The battle became one of the stark examples of the cost of frontal attacks in 1864.

siege-petersburg-1864.gloss: Union forces besieged Petersburg for months, stretching Confederate defenses around Richmond. The fall of Petersburg forced Lee retreat and led to Appomattox.

battle-cerro-cora-1870.title: Battle of Cerro Corá
battle-cerro-cora-1870.gloss: Brazilian forces killed Paraguayan president Francisco Solano López at Cerro Corá. The battle ended the Paraguayan War.
battle-cerro-cora-1870.casualties: Paraguay: 100 killed and 240 captured; Brazil: 7 wounded

battle-angamos-1879.gloss: Chile captured the Peruvian ironclad Huascar at Angamos. The battle gave Chile naval dominance in the War of the Pacific.

battle-miraflores-1881.gloss: Chilean forces defeated Peruvian defenders at Miraflores after fighting outside Lima. The victory led to the occupation of the Peruvian capital.

battle-loos-1915.gloss: British forces attacked at Loos and used poison gas for the first time. The battle produced heavy casualties for limited gains.

battle-vimy-ridge-1917.gloss: Canadian forces captured Vimy Ridge during the Arras offensive. The battle became a central moment in Canadian military memory.

battle-vittorio-veneto-1918.gloss: Italian and Allied forces broke the Austro-Hungarian army at Vittorio Veneto. The battle hastened Austria-Hungary collapse at the end of World War I.

combatants: British, Canadian, Indian, and local defenders

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-hong-kong-1941.gloss: Japanese forces captured Hong Kong after a short but hard campaign in December 1941. The battle was part of Japan opening offensive across the Pacific and Asia.

series.label: World War II in Asia and the Pacific

battle-java-sea-1942.gloss: Japanese warships defeated an Allied squadron attempting to defend the Dutch East Indies. The battle opened the way for Japan conquest of Java.

dieppe-raid-1942.gloss: Allied forces, mostly Canadian, suffered heavy losses in a raid on Dieppe. The failure influenced planning for later amphibious operations.

battle-aachen-1944.gloss: US forces captured Aachen after intense urban combat. It was the first major German city taken by the western Allies.

siege-budapest-1944.gloss: Soviet and Romanian forces besieged and captured Budapest after brutal urban fighting. The siege destroyed much of the city and weakened Axis defenses in Hungary.

battle-lake-balaton-1945.gloss: Germany launched its last major offensive near Lake Balaton in Hungary. Soviet forces stopped the attack and resumed the drive toward Vienna.

battle-abu-ageila-1967.gloss: Israeli forces broke through Egyptian defenses at Abu-Ageila in a coordinated night attack. The victory opened the route across Sinai during the Six-Day War.

battle-chinese-farm-1973.location: Sinai Peninsula near the Suez Canal
battle-chinese-farm-1973.gloss: Israeli and Egyptian forces fought around the Chinese Farm during Israel's counteroffensive in Sinai. The battle helped secure Israel's bridgehead across the Suez Canal.

battle-muye-1046bc.gloss: The Zhou army defeated the Shang near Muye and overthrew the last Shang king. The battle became a foundational episode in Chinese dynastic history.

battle-chengpu-632bc.gloss: Jin forces defeated Chu at Chengpu after a carefully staged withdrawal. The victory established Duke Wen of Jin as a leading hegemon.

battle-guiling-354bc.gloss: Qi used a feint against Wei's home territory to relieve pressure on Zhao. The battle became a classic example of attacking an enemy's vulnerable center of gravity.

battle-julu-207bc.gloss: Xiang Yu destroyed major Qin forces at Julu during the rebellions against Qin rule. The victory accelerated the dynasty's collapse.

battle-allia-390bc.gloss: A Gallic army routed Roman forces near the Allia River. The defeat opened the way for the sack of Rome and left a deep mark on Roman memory.

battle-sentinum-295bc.gloss: Rome defeated a coalition of Samnites, Gauls, and other enemies at Sentinum. The battle helped secure Roman dominance in central Italy.

battle-heraclea-280bc.gloss: Pyrrhus defeated a Roman army using Hellenistic tactics and war elephants. The battle introduced Rome to a formidable eastern Mediterranean style of warfare.

battle-asculum-279bc.gloss: Pyrrhus won again at Asculum but at severe cost. The battle gave rise to the idea of a victory so costly it undermines the victor.

battle-beneventum-275bc.gloss: Roman forces checked Pyrrhus at Beneventum and ended his Italian campaign. The result strengthened Rome's path toward control of southern Italy.

battle-mycale-479bc.gloss: Greek forces destroyed Persian naval power at Mycale on the same campaign cycle as Plataea. The victory helped shift the war into the Aegean and Asia Minor.

battle-eurymedon-466bc.gloss: Cimon won a combined land and sea victory over Persian forces near the Eurymedon. The battle confirmed Athenian-led pressure in the eastern Mediterranean.

battle-sphacteria-425bc.gloss: Athens captured Spartan hoplites on Sphacteria after fighting near Pylos. The surrender shocked the Greek world and shifted bargaining power during the Peloponnesian War.

battle-delium-424bc.gloss: Boeotian forces defeated an Athenian army near Delium. The battle showed Athens' limits on land even at the height of its imperial resources.

siege-syracuse-415bc.gloss: Athens sent a vast expedition to capture Syracuse and lost its fleet and army. The disaster weakened Athens for the final phase of the Peloponnesian War.

battle-arginusae-406bc.gloss: Athens won a major naval victory at Arginusae but failed to rescue many survivors. The political aftermath damaged Athenian command at a critical moment.

battle-gabiene-316bc.gloss: Antigonus defeated Eumenes after a hard-fought successor battle in the east. The result removed one of the last major loyalists to Alexander's royal house.

battle-sellasia-222bc.gloss: Macedonian and Achaean forces defeated reforming Sparta at Sellasia. The battle ended Cleomenes III's attempt to restore Spartan power.

battle-ilipa-206bc.gloss: Scipio Africanus defeated the main Carthaginian army in Hispania at Ilipa. The victory cleared the way for Rome to attack Carthaginian power directly.

battle-baecula-208bc.gloss: Scipio defeated Hasdrubal Barca at Baecula during the struggle for Hispania. The victory weakened Carthaginian control but did not stop Hasdrubal from marching toward Italy.

battle-mylae-260bc.gloss: Rome won its first major naval victory against Carthage at Mylae. The battle showed that Roman boarding tactics could offset Carthaginian seamanship.

battle-ecnomus-256bc.gloss: Roman and Carthaginian fleets fought one of antiquity's largest naval battles near Cape Ecnomus. Rome's victory opened the way for an invasion of Africa.

battle-drepana-249bc.gloss: Carthaginian ships destroyed a Roman fleet at Drepana. The defeat was Rome's worst naval setback of the First Punic War.

battle-arausio-105bc.gloss: Cimbri and Teutones destroyed Roman forces near Arausio after divided Roman commanders failed to cooperate. The disaster helped pave the way for Marius' military reforms.

battle-colline-gate-82bc.gloss: Sulla defeated Marian and Samnite forces at the gates of Rome. The battle secured his dictatorship and transformed Roman politics through proscriptions and constitutional changes.

battle-tigranocerta-69bc.gloss: Lucullus defeated Tigranes the Great near Tigranocerta despite being heavily outnumbered. The battle exposed the vulnerability of Armenia's expanded empire.

battle-watling-street-60.gloss: A smaller Roman army crushed Boudica's rebel forces somewhere along Watling Street. The victory secured Roman control in Britain after a major uprising.

siege-jerusalem-70.gloss: Roman forces under Titus captured Jerusalem after a devastating siege. The destruction of the Second Temple reshaped Jewish and Roman history.

battle-mons-graupius-83.gloss: Tacitus describes Agricola defeating Caledonian forces at Mons Graupius. The battle marked the high-water point of Roman campaigning in northern Britain.

battle-abrittus-251.war: Gothic invasions of the Roman Empire
battle-abrittus-251.gloss: Gothic forces defeated the Romans at Abritus, killing Emperor Decius. The battle exposed the empire's vulnerability during the third-century crisis.

battle-frigidus-394.gloss: Theodosius defeated Eugenius and Arbogast near the Frigidus River. The battle reunited the empire briefly and strengthened Nicene Christian imperial policy.

battle-uhud-625.gloss: Quraysh forces checked the Muslims near Mount Uhud after part of the Muslim line abandoned its position. The battle became a major lesson in discipline and resilience in early Islamic memory.

battle-trench-627.gloss: Medina's defenders used a trench to block a confederate siege. The failure of the siege strengthened Muhammad's position in Arabia.

battle-zab-750.gloss: Abbasid forces defeated the Umayyad army near the Zab River. The battle ended Umayyad rule in the central caliphate and opened the Abbasid era.

siege-constantinople-626.gloss: Constantinople withstood a combined Avar and Sasanian threat while Heraclius campaigned elsewhere. The failed siege preserved the Byzantine capital at a moment of extreme danger.

battle-pliska-811.gloss: Bulgar forces destroyed a Byzantine army after Emperor Nikephoros I sacked Pliska. The emperor was killed, making the battle a severe Byzantine disaster.

battle-anchialus-917.gloss: Simeon I's Bulgarians crushed a Byzantine army near Anchialus. The battle confirmed Bulgaria as a dominant Balkan power.

battle-bach-dang-938.gloss: Ngo Quyen used stakes in the Bach Dang River to destroy a Southern Han fleet. The battle became a landmark in Vietnamese independence.

battle-clontarf-1014.gloss: Brian Boru's forces defeated Leinster and Norse-allied opponents near Dublin. Brian was killed, but the battle became central to Irish historical memory.

battle-brunanburh-937.gloss: Athelstan defeated a northern coalition at Brunanburh. The battle helped secure the idea of a unified English kingdom.

battle-maldon-991.gloss: Viking raiders defeated an Anglo-Saxon force led by Byrhtnoth at Maldon. The battle became famous through an Old English poem about loyalty and defeat.

battle-dyrrachium-1081.gloss: Robert Guiscard defeated Alexios I Komnenos near Dyrrhachium. The battle opened a dangerous Norman invasion of Byzantine territory.

battle-harran-1104.gloss: Seljuk forces defeated an army from Edessa and Antioch near Harran. The battle checked early crusader expansion beyond northern Syria.

battle-beroia-1122.gloss: John II Komnenos defeated the Pechenegs near Beroia. The victory ended the Pechenegs as a major threat to Byzantium.

first-battle-tarain-1191.gloss: Prithviraj Chauhan defeated Muhammad of Ghor near Tarain in 1191. The victory temporarily checked Ghurid expansion into northern India.

second-battle-tarain-1192.gloss: Muhammad of Ghor returned to Tarain and defeated Prithviraj Chauhan. The battle opened the way for lasting Muslim-ruled polities in northern India.

battle-chandawar-1194.gloss: Ghurid forces defeated Jayachandra of the Gahadavala dynasty at Chandawar. The battle extended Ghurid control into the Ganges plain.

battle-kalka-river-1223.gloss: A Mongol reconnaissance army destroyed a Rus-Cuman coalition on the Kalka River. The battle foreshadowed the larger Mongol invasion of Rus lands.

battle-indus-1221.war: Mongol conquest of the Khwarazmian Empire
battle-indus-1221.gloss: Genghis Khan defeated Jalal al-Din near the Indus after the collapse of Khwarazm. Jalal escaped, but Mongol dominance in Central Asia was confirmed.

battle-yehuling-1211.war: Mongol conquest of the Jin dynasty
battle-yehuling-1211.gloss: Genghis Khan's army defeated a much larger Jin force at Yehuling. The battle opened northern China to sustained Mongol pressure.

siege-kiev-1240.gloss: Mongol forces stormed Kiev after a siege in 1240. The city's fall symbolized the devastation of the Mongol invasion of Rus.

siege-xiangyang-1267.gloss: Mongol forces captured Xiangyang and Fancheng after a long siege aided by counterweight trebuchets. The victory opened the route into the Song heartland.

battle-dan-no-ura-1185.gloss: The Minamoto defeated the Taira in a decisive naval battle at Dan-no-ura. The result ended the Genpei War and helped establish the Kamakura shogunate.

battle-baekgang-663.gloss: Tang and Silla forces defeated Baekje loyalists and their Yamato allies at Baekgang. The battle ended hopes of restoring Baekje.

battle-bunei-1274.gloss: Mongol Yuan forces landed near Hakata Bay during the first invasion of Japan. The expedition withdrew after fierce fighting and severe weather.
